title: Borrowing a ladder from the man across the hall
summary: A short story about a small favour from a neighbour that became a small friendship.
topic: Borrowing a ladder from the man across the hall
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a tool, ingredient, or small item you borrowed from a neighbour. Describe the situation, the borrowing, and what happened afterwards. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: Two weeks after I moved into the building in Sofia, the light in my bathroom…
I had no ladder and the ceiling was too high to reach…
I knocked on the door across the hall…
sampleText: Two weeks after I moved into a building in Sofia, the light in my bathroom went out at the worst possible moment, on a Sunday evening when no shop was open. I had no ladder, and the ceiling was much too high to reach with any chair I owned. I stood in my kitchen for a long time, trying to decide whether to wait until morning. Then I remembered the small wooden door across the hall, with its row of three faded stickers, and the older man who always nodded at me by the post boxes. I knocked. He opened the door in slippers, holding a half-eaten apple. I asked, in slow Bulgarian, if he had a ladder. He laughed once, disappeared, and came back two minutes later with a metal step ladder and a fresh light bulb. He would not take any money. While I was thanking him at the door, he asked me where I was from and told me he had been a teacher of physics for forty years. The next morning I left a small bag of clementines outside his door. After that, we said good morning by name. Borrowing a ladder costs nothing, but sometimes it pays you back for years.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đèn trong nhà tắm của tôi tắt
vào thời điểm tệ nhất có thể

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trần nhà quá cao để với tới bằng bất kỳ chiếc ghế nào tôi có

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the small wooden door across the hall
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cánh cửa gỗ nhỏ đối diện qua hành lang

sampleAnnotations.phrase: with its row of three faded stickers

sampleAnnotations.phrase: who always nodded at me by the post boxes
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: người luôn gật đầu với tôi ở chỗ hộp thư
đang cầm một quả táo ăn dở

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He would not take any money
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông nhất quyết không nhận tiền

sampleAnnotations.phrase: While I was thanking him at the door
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Trong khi tôi đang cảm ơn ông ở cửa

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he had been a teacher of physics for forty years
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ông đã làm giáo viên vật lý trong bốn mươi năm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Borrowing a ladder costs nothing, but sometimes it pays you back for years

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Would not' for past refusal
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'He would not take any money' dùng 'would not' (refused to) chỉ sự từ chối kiên quyết trong quá khứ, không phải điều kiện.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'He would not take any money' uses 'would not' (refused to) to express a firm past refusal, not a conditional.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for prior career
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'he had been a teacher of physics for forty years' — past perfect ('had been + for + duration') chỉ một quãng nghề nghiệp kéo dài đến trước thời điểm cuộc trò chuyện.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'He had been a teacher of physics for forty years' — past perfect ('had been + for + duration') marks a career span reaching up to (but before) the past moment of conversation.

sampleTranslationVi: Hai tuần sau khi tôi chuyển vào một tòa nhà ở Sofia, đèn trong nhà tắm của tôi tắt vào thời điểm tệ nhất có thể, một tối Chủ Nhật khi không có cửa hàng nào mở. Tôi không có thang, và trần nhà quá cao để với tới bằng bất kỳ chiếc ghế nào tôi có. Tôi đứng trong bếp một lúc lâu, cố quyết định có nên đợi đến sáng không. Sau đó tôi nhớ đến cánh cửa gỗ nhỏ đối diện qua hành lang, với hàng ba sticker đã phai màu, và ông già luôn gật đầu với tôi ở chỗ hộp thư. Tôi gõ cửa. Ông mở cửa trong đôi dép lê, đang cầm một quả táo ăn dở. Tôi hỏi, bằng tiếng Bulgaria chậm, ông có thang không. Ông cười một tiếng, biến mất, và quay lại sau hai phút với một chiếc thang gấp kim loại và một bóng đèn mới. Ông nhất quyết không nhận tiền. Trong khi tôi đang cảm ơn ông ở cửa, ông hỏi tôi đến từ đâu và kể rằng ông đã làm giáo viên vật lý trong bốn mươi năm. Sáng hôm sau tôi để một túi nhỏ quýt clementine ngoài cửa ông. Sau đó, chúng tôi chào nhau buổi sáng bằng tên. Mượn một chiếc thang không tốn gì cả, nhưng đôi khi nó trả lại bạn trong nhiều năm.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence.
options: The light in my bathroom go out at the worst possible moment.
The light in my bathroom was went out at the worst possible moment.
The light in my bathroom went out at the worst possible moment.
The light in my bathroom has gone out at the worst possible moment.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'went out' for a finished past event.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'too + adjective + to + infinitive'.
options: The ceiling was much too high to reaching with any chair I owned.
The ceiling was much too high for reach with any chair I owned.
The ceiling was much too high to reached with any chair I owned.
The ceiling was much too high to reach with any chair I owned.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Too + adjective + to + base verb' is the fixed pattern.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the difference between 'borrow' and 'lend'?
options: 'Borrow' = take from someone; 'lend' = give to someone temporarily
Both words mean exactly the same thing
'Borrow' = give; 'lend' = take
'Borrow' is for money only; 'lend' is for objects only
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I borrow X from you' = you give X to me temporarily. 'I lend X to you' = same direction, opposite subject.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'would not' for past refusal.
options: He would not took any money.
He would not take any money.
He would not taking any money.
He would not to take any money.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Would not + base verb' = refused to + verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While I thanking him at the door, he asked me where I was from.
While I thank him at the door, he asked me where I was from.
While I was thanking him at the door, he asked me where I was from.
While I am thanking him at the door, he asked me where I was from.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was thanking' for background; past simple 'asked' for the inserted question.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect sentence about career.
options: He has been a teacher of physics for forty years.
He was been a teacher of physics for forty years.
He had being a teacher of physics for forty years.
He had been a teacher of physics for forty years.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had been + for + duration' for a span ending before the past moment.

options: on the opposite side of the corridor
next door on the same side
two floors above
in the basement
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Across the hall' = directly opposite, separated by the corridor.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with reported indirect question.
options: He asked me where was I from.
He asked me where I was from.
He asked me where I am from.
He asked me where do I from.
mcQuestions.explanation: Reported indirect question keeps statement word order ('I was'), with backshift to past tense.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about leaving a gift.
options: I left a small bag of clementine outside his door.
I left a small bag of clementines outside his door yesterday morning.
I left a small bag of clementines outside his door.
I left a small bag from clementines outside his door.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about saying hello with names.
options: After that, we said good morning with name.
After that, we said good morning at name.
After that, we said good morning to name.
After that, we said good morning by name.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'By name' = using the name. Fixed prepositional phrase.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural closing line.
options: Borrowing a ladder costs nothing, but sometimes it pays you back for years.
Borrow a ladder costs nothing, but sometimes it pays you back for years.
Borrowing a ladder cost nothing, but sometimes it pays you back for years.
Borrowing a ladder costs nothing, but sometimes it pay you back for years.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why does a single small favour from a neighbour often open a door wider than weeks of polite hellos?
